One authority cue that is worth highlighting is not something material, such as the car you drive or the mobile phone you use or the clothes you wear. Instead, it involves speaking with confidence and conviction in a tone that reinforces your expertise and, as research shows, increases your influence. At the same time, it's worth recognizing that although using professional jargon can activate a perception of specialized expertise, it has a downside. Using jargon with people who don't understand it may confuse them or make them see you as trying to act superior to them. So with an audience of non-specialists, retain a confident tone but avoid the use of jargon.
